California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Bruce W. Robeck 
Paralegal/Administrator 
Political Reports unit 
Nielsen, Mersksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
770 L street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Robeck: 
July 14, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-359 
This is to confirm the telephone advice given to you 
concerning the Political Reform Act1 / by Jeanne Pritchard of this 
office on June 7, 1989. 
Specifically, Ms. Pritchard informed you that contributions 
by a husband and wife do not have to be cumulated for purposes of 
disclosure on a campaign statement. 
This advice is based on the 
assumption that the recipient of the contributions was informed in 
writing at the time the contributions were received that a portion 
of the contributions were from the husband and a portion from the 
wife. 
For example, separate checks were received, one signed by 
the husband and one signed by the wife. 
Alternatively, this 
information could be provided with one check signed by either 
husband or wife, accompanied by a letter signed by both indicating 
the apportionment of contributions between them. 
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
KED:SH:ld 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, 
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER 
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORr,bJlATicms 
770 L STREET, SUITE 800'~"d Fe;'

June 14, 1989 
Chief, Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Jeanne: 
FILE NUMBER 
This letter is to confirm in writing my understanding 
of the telephone advice you provided to me on Wednesday, June 7, 
1989. 
QUESTION 
Are contributions by a husband and a wife made from 
community property funds but with independent decision making by 
each spouse cumulated for purposes of disclosure on the 
recipients campaign statement? 
SUMMARY OF ADVICE 
Contributions by a husband and a wife are not cumulated 
for purposes of disclosure on a campaign statement. 
If the above information conforms to your understanding 
of the question and the answer, please confirm. 
BWR/rh 
Sincerely, 
BRUCE W. ROBECK 
Paralegal/Administrator 
Political Reports Unit

California

June 19, 1989 
Bruce h'. Robeck 
Paralegal/Administrator 
Political Reports unit 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
770 L street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Robeck: 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-359 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on June 15, 1989. 
Your letter has 
been assigned to Scott Hallabrin for response. 
If you have any 
questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to ex~dite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadvl 
Sincerely, 
E. Donovan
